perturbation.original_text: 1. California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043 provide rights to you concerning termination or non-renewal of a franchise. If the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the law will control.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. California Business and Professions Code Sections 20000 through 20043 provide rights to you concerning termination or non-renewal of a franchise. Even if the Master Franchise Agreement contains provisions that are inconsistent with the law, the terms of the Master Franchise Agreement shall still govern.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Master Franchise Agreement's terms will govern even if inconsistent with California law, directly contradicting the original statement that the law will control. This introduces uncertainty about which set of rules applies, leading to potential disputes. This perturbation contradicts item 7.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The Master Franchise Agreement requires application of the laws of New York. This provision may not be enforceable under California law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Master Franchise Agreement governs even if inconsistent with California law. The contradicted text (item 7) raises concerns about the enforceability of the New York law provision under California law. By asserting the agreement's dominance regardless of legal inconsistencies, the changed text directly contradicts the potential unenforceability highlighted in item 7, creating a significant in-text contradiction. This directly violates the guidelines for Inconsistencies in Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. The Master Franchise Agreement requires non-binding mediation. The mediation will occur in New York with the costs being borne by equally by the parties. Prospective franchisees are encouraged to consult private legal counsel to determine the applicability of California and federal laws (such as Business and Professions Code Section 20040.5 and Code of Civil Procedure Section 1281) to any provisions of a franchise agreement restricting venue to a forum outside the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. The Master Franchise Agreement requires mandatory and binding mediation. The mediation will occur in New York with the costs being borne entirely by the franchisee. Prospective franchisees are discouraged from consulting private legal counsel, as the terms of this agreement are final and non-negotiable.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing 'non-binding' mediation to 'mandatory and binding' mediation, shifting the cost burden to the franchisee, and discouraging legal counsel. This creates conflict and uncertainty regarding the franchisee's rights and obligations. The original text allows for non-binding mediation with shared costs, and encourages franchisees to seek legal counsel to understand their rights regarding venue restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. You must sign a general release if you renew or transfer your franchise. California Corporation Code 31512 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Investment Law (California Corporations Code 31000 through 31516). Business and Professions Code 20010 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Relations Act (Business and Professions Code 20000 through 20043).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several elements that strongly contradict the contradicted text. Specifically, the shift from 'non-binding mediation' to 'mandatory and binding mediation' and the discouragement of legal counsel directly impede the franchisee's ability to assert rights under California law, which the contradicted text explicitly protects against waiver, as stated in California Corporation Code 31512 and Business and Professions Code 20010.

perturbation.original_text: 9. THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE INVESTMENT LAW REQUIRES THAT A COPY OF ALL PROPOSED AGREEMENTS RELATING TO THE SALE OF THE FRANCHISE BE DELIVERED TOGETHER WITH THE DISCLOSURE DOCUMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. THE CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE INVESTMENT LAW REQUIRES THAT A COPY OF ALL PROPOSED AGREEMENTS RELATING TO THE SALE OF THE FRANCHISE BE DELIVERED TOGETHER WITH THE DISCLOSURE DOCUMENT. However, this requirement is waived if the franchisee acknowledges they have received and understood the disclosure document.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a waiver clause to the requirement for delivering proposed agreements with the disclosure document. The original text states a clear requirement, while the added clause allows for a waiver based on franchisee acknowledgment. This creates ambiguity and undermines the purpose of the disclosure requirement, potentially harming the franchisee's ability to make informed decisions. This will contradict item 8 as well, due to it limiting the rights afforded in item 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. You must sign a general release if you renew or transfer your franchise. California Corporation Code 31512 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Investment Law (California Corporations Code 31000 through 31516). Business and Professions Code 20010 voids a waiver of your rights under the Franchise Relations Act (Business and Professions Code 20000 through 20043).
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 9 states a mandatory requirement for delivering proposed agreements. The changed text introduces a waiver to this requirement based on franchisee acknowledgment. This directly contradicts section 8, which voids waivers of rights under the Franchise Investment Law. The change weakens the protections afforded under existing laws as articulated in section 8 by allowing franchisees to unknowingly waive protections by acknowledging they have received and understood the disclosure document.
